metadata.description: A practical AI recipe library for small business owners.

categories.name: Customer Communication
categories.description: Emails, customer replies, follow-ups, reviews and more.

categories.name: Sales & Quotes
categories.description: Quotes, proposals, offers, follow-ups and sales scripts.

categories.name: Marketing
categories.description: Social media, website copy, newsletters and campaigns.

categories.name: Operations & Admin
categories.description: Meetings, SOPs, checklists, planning and internal processes.

categories.name: Finance & Payments
categories.description: Invoice reminders, pricing explanations, budgets and expense review.

categories.name: HR & Team
categories.description: Job descriptions, interviews, feedback, training and team updates.

tools.name: ChatGPT
tools.description: Best general-purpose AI tool for everyday business writing and task support.

tools.name: Claude
tools.description: Useful for longer documents and careful writing tasks.

tools.name: Gemini
tools.description: Useful for businesses already using Google tools.

tools.name: Perplexity
tools.description: Best for research-style tasks where current sources matter.

tools.name: Canva AI
tools.description: Useful for creating simple business visuals without design experience.

workflows.title: Prepare a Quote
workflows.description: Go from customer enquiry to quote and follow-up.

workflows.title: Reply to an Unhappy Customer
workflows.description: Handle a complaint professionally and protect your reputation.

workflows.title: Create Weekly Marketing
workflows.description: Plan and write useful marketing content for the week.

workflows.title: Chase an Overdue Payment
workflows.description: Send payment reminders without damaging the customer relationship.

workflows.title: Document a Business Process
workflows.description: Turn repeatable work into a simple SOP or checklist.

sections.heading: Welcome
sections.body: This toolkit is designed for busy small business owners who want to use AI in a practical way, without becoming AI experts.

sections.heading: What You Need
sections.body: A device, internet access, a free ChatGPT, Claude or Gemini account, and a real business task you want to complete.

sections.heading: Your First AI Win
sections.body: Open ChatGPT, Claude or Gemini choose one recipe, paste your business context, review the result, and use it.

sections.heading: The Sophia Formula
sections.body: Role, context, goal, constraints, and output. This structure helps AI give better business-ready responses.

sections.heading: Before You Send Anything
sections.body: Always check names, dates, prices, tone, promises, and private information before sending AI-generated content.

recipes.title: Reply to a Customer Enquiry
recipes.description: Reply quickly and professionally to a new customer.
recipes.purpose: Reply quickly and professionally to a new customer.
recipes.whenToUse: When someone asks about your service, pricing, or availability.
recipes.prompt:
Act as a professional customer service assistant.

My business is: [BUSINESS TYPE]
The customer message is: [PASTE MESSAGE]

Write a friendly and professional reply.
Answer their question clearly.
Ask for any missing information I need to help them.
Keep it simple and easy to understand.
beforeUse: Personalise the final message before sending.

recipes.title: Reply to a Customer Complaint
recipes.description: Respond calmly to an unhappy customer.
recipes.purpose: Respond calmly to an unhappy customer.
recipes.whenToUse: When a customer complains by email, message, review, or social media.
recipes.prompt:
Act as a professional customer service manager.

A customer has sent this complaint: [PASTE COMPLAINT]

Write a polite response.
Acknowledge the issue.
Apologise for the inconvenience.
Do not admit legal responsibility.
Do not offer a refund unless I clearly include one.
Keep the tone calm, respectful, and professional.
beforeUse: Confirm what actually happened before sending.
Do not make promises you cannot keep.
Escalate serious legal or safety issues.

recipes.title: Follow Up With a Lead
recipes.description: Follow up with someone who showed interest but has not replied.
recipes.purpose: Follow up with someone who showed interest but has not replied.
recipes.whenToUse: When a potential customer has gone quiet after an enquiry, call, or quote.
recipes.prompt:
Act as a sales assistant.

Write a short follow-up message to this potential customer: [PASTE CONTEXT]

The goal is to politely check if they are still interested.
Keep it friendly, not pushy.
Include a simple call to action.
beforeUse: Mention the specific product or service.

recipes.title: Write a Professional Email
recipes.description: Turn rough notes into a polished business email.
recipes.purpose: Turn rough notes into a polished business email.
recipes.whenToUse: When you know what to say but need help making it clear and professional.
recipes.prompt:
Rewrite the following notes as a clear, professional email:

[PASTE NOTES]

Keep the tone: [friendly / formal / direct]
Make it easy to read.
Remove unnecessary words.
Do not change the meaning.
beforeUse: Confirm the message still says what you intended.

recipes.title: Shorten an Email
recipes.description: Make a long email shorter and clearer.
recipes.purpose: Make a long email shorter and clearer.
recipes.whenToUse: When an email is too long, repetitive, or hard to read.
recipes.prompt:
Shorten this email while keeping it professional and clear:

[PASTE EMAIL]

Keep the main message.
Remove repetition.
Make it easier to read.
beforeUse: Keep required details like dates and prices.
Use your own greeting and sign-off.

recipes.title: Make an Email More Friendly
recipes.description: Improve the tone of an email without sounding too casual.
recipes.purpose: Improve the tone of an email without sounding too casual.
recipes.whenToUse: When an email sounds too cold, direct, or formal.
recipes.prompt:
Rewrite this email to sound warmer, friendlier, and still professional:

[PASTE EMAIL]

Keep the same meaning.
Do not make it too long.
beforeUse: Make sure it still matches your brand voice.
Check the message is still clear.

recipes.title: Write an Appointment Reminder
recipes.description: Remind customers about appointments.
recipes.purpose: Remind customers about appointments.
recipes.whenToUse: Before appointments, bookings, consultations, inspections, or service visits.
recipes.prompt:
Write a short appointment reminder message.

Business: [BUSINESS NAME]
Customer: [CUSTOMER NAME]
Appointment date/time: [DATE/TIME]
Location or link: [LOCATION/LINK]

Keep it friendly and clear.
Include what the customer needs to prepare, if anything.

recipes.title: Ask a Customer for Missing Information
recipes.description: Request missing details without sounding difficult.
recipes.purpose: Request missing details without sounding difficult.
recipes.whenToUse: When you cannot proceed because the customer has not provided enough information.
recipes.prompt:
Write a polite message asking the customer for the following missing information:

[MISSING INFORMATION]

Context: [EXPLAIN SITUATION]

Keep it clear, friendly, and easy to reply to.
beforeUse: List only the information you really need.
Make it easy for the customer to answer.
Avoid sounding like the customer made a mistake.

recipes.title: Reply to a Price Objection
recipes.description: Handle “too expensive” responses professionally.
recipes.purpose: Handle “too expensive” responses professionally.
recipes.whenToUse: When a customer questions the price or compares you to a cheaper option.
recipes.prompt:
Act as a sales consultant.

A customer says our price is too expensive.

Our service/product is: [SERVICE/PRODUCT]
Price: [PRICE]
Value/benefits: [BENEFITS]

Write a professional reply that explains the value, stays respectful, and does not sound defensive.
beforeUse: Do not argue with the customer.

recipes.title: Ask for a Review
recipes.description: Ask a happy customer to leave a review.
recipes.purpose: Ask a happy customer to leave a review.
recipes.whenToUse: After completing a job, delivery, service, or sale successfully.
recipes.prompt:
Write a short message asking a happy customer to leave a review.

Business: [BUSINESS NAME]
Customer experience: [WHAT HAPPENED]
Review link: [LINK]

Keep it warm, grateful, and simple.

recipes.title: Write a Quote
recipes.description: Create a clear quotation for a customer.
recipes.purpose: Create a clear quotation for a customer.
recipes.whenToUse: When a customer asks for pricing or approves a scope discussion.
recipes.prompt:
Act as a professional business assistant.

Create a customer quote using this information:

Customer: [CUSTOMER NAME]
Service/product: [SERVICE]
Scope: [SCOPE]
Price: [PRICE]
Inclusions: [INCLUSIONS]
Exclusions: [EXCLUSIONS]
Validity: [VALIDITY PERIOD]

Make it clear, professional, and easy for the customer to understand.

recipes.title: Improve a Quote
recipes.description: Make a quote easier to understand and approve.
recipes.purpose: Make a quote easier to understand and approve.
recipes.whenToUse: When your quote feels too technical, unclear, or unpersuasive.
recipes.prompt:
Improve this quote so it is clearer and more professional:

[PASTE QUOTE]

Make the value easy to understand.
Keep the wording simple.
Do not change prices or terms.
beforeUse: Confirm AI did not change prices.

recipes.title: Write a Proposal
recipes.description: Create a simple business proposal.
recipes.purpose: Create a simple business proposal.
recipes.whenToUse: When a customer needs more detail than a quote.
recipes.prompt:
Act as a business proposal writer.

Create a proposal for:

Client: [CLIENT]
Problem: [PROBLEM]
Recommended solution: [SOLUTION]
Scope: [SCOPE]
Timeline: [TIMELINE]
Price: [PRICE]
Next step: [NEXT STEP]

Use clear sections and professional language.
beforeUse: Add your terms and conditions if required.

recipes.title: Create a Service Description
recipes.description: Explain what your service includes.
recipes.purpose: Explain what your service includes.
recipes.whenToUse: When creating website copy, brochures, proposals, or product pages.
recipes.prompt:
Write a clear service description for:

Service: [SERVICE NAME]
Business: [BUSINESS TYPE]
Target customer: [CUSTOMER TYPE]
Main benefits: [BENEFITS]

Make it suitable for a website or brochure.

recipes.title: Create a Sales Script
recipes.description: Prepare for a customer call.
recipes.purpose: Prepare for a customer call.
recipes.whenToUse: Before discovery calls, phone enquiries, demos, or sales meetings.
recipes.prompt:
Create a simple sales call script for my business.

Business: [BUSINESS]
Service/product: [SERVICE]
Target customer: [CUSTOMER]
Main problem solved: [PROBLEM]

Include opening, questions, value explanation, objection handling, and closing.
beforeUse: Do not read it like a robot.

recipes.title: Create a Follow-Up Sequence
recipes.description: Follow up after sending a quote.
recipes.purpose: Follow up after sending a quote.
recipes.whenToUse: When a customer has received a quote but has not replied.
recipes.prompt:
Create a 3-message follow-up sequence for a customer who received a quote but has not replied.

Service: [SERVICE]
Tone: friendly and professional
Goal: encourage a response without being pushy

Include messages for day 2, day 5, and day 10.
beforeUse: Set reminders to send each follow-up.

recipes.title: Write a Discount Response
recipes.description: Reply when a customer asks for a discount.
recipes.purpose: Reply when a customer asks for a discount.
recipes.whenToUse: When a customer asks for a lower price or special deal.
recipes.prompt:
Write a professional response to a customer asking for a discount.

Context: [CONTEXT]
Can we offer discount? [YES/NO]
Alternative value we can offer: [OPTIONAL]

Keep the response respectful and business-focused.
beforeUse: Offer value instead of price cuts where possible.

recipes.title: Create a Product or Service Comparison
recipes.description: Help customers choose between options.
recipes.purpose: Help customers choose between options.
recipes.whenToUse: When you offer multiple packages, products, or service levels.
recipes.prompt:
Create a simple comparison table for these options:

[OPTION 1]
[OPTION 2]
[OPTION 3]

Compare price, best for, features, limitations, and recommendation.

recipes.title: Write a Why Choose Us Section
recipes.description: Improve website or proposal copy.
recipes.purpose: Improve website or proposal copy.
recipes.whenToUse: When explaining why customers should choose your business.
recipes.prompt:
Write a “Why Choose Us” section for my business.

Business: [BUSINESS]
Target customer: [CUSTOMER]
Strengths: [STRENGTHS]
Tone: professional, simple, trustworthy

recipes.title: Create an Offer
recipes.description: Package a service into a clear offer.
recipes.purpose: Package a service into a clear offer.
recipes.whenToUse: When you want to sell a service with a clear promise and inclusions.
recipes.prompt:
Help me create a clear business offer.

Business: [BUSINESS]
Service/product: [SERVICE]
Target customer: [CUSTOMER]
Problem solved: [PROBLEM]
Price: [PRICE]

Include offer name, promise, inclusions, bonuses, guarantee idea, and call to action.
beforeUse: Make sure the offer is profitable.
Do not include guarantees you cannot support.

recipes.title: Create 30 Social Media Ideas
recipes.description: Generate simple content ideas quickly.
recipes.purpose: Generate simple content ideas quickly.
recipes.whenToUse: When you do not know what to post.
recipes.prompt:
Create 30 social media post ideas for my business.

Business: [BUSINESS]
Target audience: [AUDIENCE]
Goal: [awareness / leads / trust / sales]
Platform: [Facebook / Instagram / TikTok / LinkedIn]

Make the ideas simple, practical, and easy to create.
beforeUse: Choose ideas you can actually produce.

recipes.title: Write a Facebook Post
recipes.description: Create a simple Facebook post.
recipes.purpose: Create a simple Facebook post.
recipes.whenToUse: When promoting an offer, sharing an update, or building awareness.
recipes.prompt:
Write a Facebook post for my business.

Business: [BUSINESS]
Topic/offer: [TOPIC]
Audience: [AUDIENCE]
Goal: [GOAL]

Use simple language, a friendly tone, and a clear call to action.
beforeUse: Add images or links if needed.
Make it sound like your brand.

recipes.title: Write an Instagram Caption
recipes.description: Create a short Instagram caption.
recipes.purpose: Create a short Instagram caption.
recipes.whenToUse: When posting a photo, reel, carousel, or business update.
recipes.prompt:
Write an Instagram caption for:

Business: [BUSINESS]
Post topic: [TOPIC]
Audience: [AUDIENCE]

Make it short, engaging, and natural.
Include a call to action and relevant hashtags.
beforeUse: Avoid too many emojis if not on brand.
Match the caption to the image or video.

recipes.title: Create a Content Calendar
recipes.description: Plan 4 weeks of content.
recipes.purpose: Plan 4 weeks of content.
recipes.whenToUse: When you want a simple posting plan for the month.
recipes.prompt:
Create a 4-week content calendar for my business.

Business: [BUSINESS]
Audience: [AUDIENCE]
Main services/products: [SERVICES]
Platforms: [PLATFORMS]

Include post topic, caption idea, content format, and goal.

recipes.title: Rewrite Website Homepage Copy
recipes.description: Make homepage copy clearer and more persuasive.
recipes.purpose: Make homepage copy clearer and more persuasive.
recipes.whenToUse: When your website does not clearly explain what you do.
recipes.prompt:
Rewrite my homepage copy to make it clearer and more persuasive.

Current copy: [PASTE COPY]
Business: [BUSINESS]
Target customer: [CUSTOMER]
Main offer: [OFFER]

Make it simple, trustworthy, and action-focused.
beforeUse: Add location and services where relevant.

recipes.title: Create a Google Business Profile Description
recipes.description: Write a local business description.
recipes.purpose: Write a local business description.
recipes.whenToUse: When setting up or improving your Google Business Profile.
recipes.prompt:
Write a Google Business Profile description.

Business name: [NAME]
Business type: [TYPE]
Location: [LOCATION]
Services: [SERVICES]
Ideal customer: [CUSTOMER]

Keep it professional, local, and easy to understand.

recipes.title: Reply to a Positive Review
recipes.description: Respond warmly to good reviews.
recipes.purpose: Respond warmly to good reviews.
recipes.whenToUse: When a customer leaves a positive review online.
recipes.prompt:
Write a warm reply to this positive review:

[PASTE REVIEW]

Business: [BUSINESS NAME]
Keep it short, grateful, and personal.
beforeUse: Use the customer's name if appropriate.
Mention something specific from the review.

recipes.title: Reply to a Negative Review
recipes.description: Respond professionally to negative reviews.
recipes.purpose: Respond professionally to negative reviews.
recipes.whenToUse: When your business receives a public complaint or bad review.
recipes.prompt:
Write a professional reply to this negative review:

[PASTE REVIEW]

Acknowledge the concern.
Stay calm.
Do not argue.
Invite the customer to contact us privately.
Protect the business reputation.
beforeUse: Do not share private details publicly.
Ask a manager to review serious cases.

recipes.title: Create an Email Newsletter
recipes.description: Write a simple email newsletter.
recipes.purpose: Write a simple email newsletter.
recipes.whenToUse: When you want to update customers or promote something by email.
recipes.prompt:
Write a simple email newsletter.

Business: [BUSINESS]
Topic: [TOPIC]
Audience: [AUDIENCE]
Goal: [GOAL]

Include subject line, short intro, main message, and call to action.
beforeUse: Send only to people who opted in where required.

recipes.title: Create a Simple Marketing Campaign
recipes.description: Plan a short promotional campaign.
recipes.purpose: Plan a short promotional campaign.
recipes.whenToUse: When launching an offer, event, product, or service.
recipes.prompt:
Create a simple 7-day marketing campaign for my business.

Business: [BUSINESS]
Offer: [OFFER]
Audience: [AUDIENCE]
Platform: [PLATFORM]

Include daily message idea, content format, and call to action.

recipes.title: Summarise Meeting Notes
recipes.description: Turn messy notes into a clear summary.
recipes.purpose: Turn messy notes into a clear summary.
recipes.whenToUse: After meetings, calls, workshops, or site visits.
recipes.prompt:
Summarise these meeting notes:

[PASTE NOTES]

Include:
- Key points
- Decisions made
- Action items
- Owner for each action
- Deadlines, if mentioned

recipes.title: Create a Meeting Agenda
recipes.description: Prepare a clear agenda for a meeting.
recipes.purpose: Prepare a clear agenda for a meeting.
recipes.whenToUse: Before team meetings, client meetings, planning sessions, or reviews.
recipes.prompt:
Create a meeting agenda.

Meeting topic: [TOPIC]
Attendees: [ATTENDEES]
Goal: [GOAL]
Time available: [TIME]

Include agenda items, estimated time, and desired outcome.
beforeUse: Keep agenda realistic for the time available.

recipes.title: Create an SOP
recipes.description: Create a simple Standard Operating Procedure.
recipes.purpose: Create a simple Standard Operating Procedure.
recipes.whenToUse: When you need a repeatable process for your team.
recipes.prompt:
Create a simple Standard Operating Procedure for this task:

Task: [TASK]
Business: [BUSINESS]
Who performs it: [ROLE]
Tools needed: [TOOLS]

Include purpose, steps, quality checks, and common mistakes.
beforeUse: Test the SOP with a real task.
Ask the team to review it.
Update it after mistakes or changes.

recipes.title: Improve an Existing Process
recipes.description: Find ways to save time and reduce mistakes.
recipes.purpose: Find ways to save time and reduce mistakes.
recipes.whenToUse: When a current process feels slow, manual, or confusing.
recipes.prompt:
Review this business process and suggest improvements:

[PASTE PROCESS]

Business: [BUSINESS]
Goal: save time, reduce mistakes, and improve customer experience.

Give practical recommendations.
beforeUse: Do not automate a broken process too early.

recipes.title: Create a Checklist
recipes.description: Create a practical checklist for repeatable tasks.
recipes.purpose: Create a practical checklist for repeatable tasks.
recipes.whenToUse: When you want a task done consistently.
recipes.prompt:
Create a checklist for this business task:

Task: [TASK]
Business: [BUSINESS]
Frequency: [daily / weekly / monthly / per customer]

Make it clear, practical, and easy to follow.
beforeUse: Keep the checklist short enough to use.
Test it on a real job.

recipes.title: Create a Weekly Plan
recipes.description: Organise business priorities for the week.
recipes.purpose: Organise business priorities for the week.
recipes.whenToUse: At the start of the week or when work feels scattered.
recipes.prompt:
Create a weekly business plan.

Business: [BUSINESS]
Main priorities: [PRIORITIES]
Tasks to complete: [TASKS]
Time available: [TIME]

Organise tasks by day and priority.

recipes.title: Prioritise Tasks
recipes.description: Decide what to do first.
recipes.purpose: Decide what to do first.
recipes.whenToUse: When you have too many tasks and need focus.
recipes.prompt:
Help me prioritise this task list:

[PASTE TASKS]

Use urgency, business impact, and effort required.
Create a clear priority order.

recipes.title: Write Internal Instructions
recipes.description: Create clear instructions for your team.
recipes.purpose: Create clear instructions for your team.
recipes.whenToUse: When delegating or explaining how to complete a task.
recipes.prompt:
Write clear internal instructions for my team.

Task/process: [TASK]
Audience: [TEAM/ROLE]
Important rules: [RULES]

Use simple steps and clear language.

recipes.title: Create a Handover Note
recipes.description: Help someone else continue your work.
recipes.purpose: Help someone else continue your work.
recipes.whenToUse: When passing work to another person, shift, contractor, or team member.
recipes.prompt:
Create a handover note.

Context: [CONTEXT]
Current status: [STATUS]
Pending tasks: [PENDING]
Risks/issues: [RISKS]
Next steps: [NEXT STEPS]

Make it clear for someone else to continue the work.
beforeUse: Include links or files if relevant.

recipes.title: Create a Business Decision Summary
recipes.description: Structure a business decision clearly.
recipes.purpose: Structure a business decision clearly.
recipes.whenToUse: When comparing options or deciding what to do next.
recipes.prompt:
Help me make a business decision.

Decision: [DECISION]
Options: [OPTIONS]
Pros and cons: [DETAILS]
Important constraints: [CONSTRAINTS]

Create a summary with recommendation, risks, and next steps.
beforeUse: Check financial or legal details separately.

recipes.title: Write an Invoice Reminder
recipes.description: Send a friendly payment reminder.
recipes.purpose: Send a friendly payment reminder.
recipes.whenToUse: When an invoice is due or close to due.
recipes.prompt:
Write a friendly payment reminder.

Customer: [CUSTOMER]
Invoice number: [INVOICE]
Amount: [AMOUNT]
Due date: [DUE DATE]

Keep it polite, professional, and clear.

recipes.title: Write a Stronger Payment Reminder
recipes.description: Send a firmer overdue payment reminder.
recipes.purpose: Send a firmer overdue payment reminder.
recipes.whenToUse: When payment is overdue and previous reminders were ignored.
recipes.prompt:
Write a firm but professional overdue payment reminder.

Customer: [CUSTOMER]
Invoice: [INVOICE]
Amount: [AMOUNT]
Days overdue: [DAYS]

Keep it respectful but clear that payment is overdue.
beforeUse: Check payment was not already received.

recipes.title: Explain Pricing to a Customer
recipes.description: Explain your pricing clearly.
recipes.purpose: Explain your pricing clearly.
recipes.whenToUse: When a customer asks why something costs what it costs.
recipes.prompt:
Help me explain my pricing clearly.

Service/product: [SERVICE]
Price: [PRICE]
What is included: [INCLUSIONS]
Why it is valuable: [VALUE]

Write a customer-friendly explanation.

recipes.title: Create a Simple Budget
recipes.description: Create a basic business budget structure.
recipes.purpose: Create a basic business budget structure.
recipes.whenToUse: When planning monthly income, costs, and profit.
recipes.prompt:
Create a simple monthly budget structure for my business.

Business type: [BUSINESS]
Income sources: [INCOME]
Main expenses: [EXPENSES]

Organise it into income, fixed costs, variable costs, and profit estimate.
beforeUse: Ask an accountant for tax or compliance decisions.

recipes.title: Analyse Expenses
recipes.description: Organise expenses and identify review areas.
recipes.purpose: Organise expenses and identify review areas.
recipes.whenToUse: When reviewing spending or preparing information for your accountant.
recipes.prompt:
Review these business expenses and organise them into categories:

[PASTE EXPENSES]

Group them by category and identify possible areas to review or reduce.

Do not provide financial advice. Keep it practical.
beforeUse: Do not rely on AI for tax treatment.

recipes.title: Write a Job Description
recipes.description: Create a clear job description.
recipes.purpose: Create a clear job description.
recipes.whenToUse: When hiring a new employee, contractor, or casual worker.
recipes.prompt:
Write a job description.

Role: [ROLE]
Business: [BUSINESS]
Responsibilities: [RESPONSIBILITIES]
Required skills: [SKILLS]
Location/work type: [LOCATION]

Make it clear, professional, and attractive to candidates.

recipes.title: Create Interview Questions
recipes.description: Prepare practical interview questions.
recipes.purpose: Prepare practical interview questions.
recipes.whenToUse: Before interviewing candidates.
recipes.prompt:
Create interview questions for this role:

Role: [ROLE]
Business: [BUSINESS]
Key skills needed: [SKILLS]

Include practical, behavioural, and role-specific questions.

recipes.title: Write Employee Feedback
recipes.description: Write respectful and constructive feedback.
recipes.purpose: Write respectful and constructive feedback.
recipes.whenToUse: When giving performance feedback or addressing an issue.
recipes.prompt:
Help me write constructive employee feedback.

Employee situation: [SITUATION]
What went well: [POSITIVE]
What needs improvement: [IMPROVEMENT]
Desired outcome: [OUTCOME]

Keep it respectful, clear, and professional.
beforeUse: Seek HR/legal advice for serious matters.

recipes.title: Create Training Instructions
recipes.description: Create simple training material for a team member.
recipes.purpose: Create simple training material for a team member.
recipes.whenToUse: When onboarding someone or teaching a task.
recipes.prompt:
Create simple training instructions for a new team member.

Role: [ROLE]
Task: [TASK]
Business: [BUSINESS]

Include step-by-step instructions, common mistakes, and quality checks.
beforeUse: Test instructions with a real person.
Add screenshots or examples if possible.

recipes.title: Write a Team Announcement
recipes.description: Write a clear team update or announcement.
recipes.purpose: Write a clear team update or announcement.
recipes.whenToUse: When informing your team about changes, updates, reminders, or actions.
recipes.prompt:
Write a team announcement.

Topic: [TOPIC]
Audience: [TEAM]
Main message: [MESSAGE]
Action required: [ACTION]

Keep it clear, professional, and easy to understand.
beforeUse: Choose the right channel to send it.
